title: Diasperus rupestris
taxonomy: Diasperus rupestris is a species of plant, and based on available information, it is classified within the family Cactaceae. This family is commonly known as cacti and includes plants with thick stems or stems covered with spines. The genus Diasperus is often grouped with other cacti genera, such as Pediocactus and Notocactus. The classification of Diasperus rupestris may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a species of cactus. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of this plant.

benefits: Diasperus rupestris, also known as the desert rockjasmine or desert night-blooming jasmine, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties.
